title: Swissto12 to build small optical relay GEO satellite for Space Compass
summary: Swissto12 will construct a small optical relay geostationary satellite for Space Compass using its HummingSat platform. This marks a significant step in optical communication technology for space applications.

title: SpaceX offers details on orbital data center satellites
summary: SpaceX CEO Elon Musk shared technical details about the company's plans to deploy a constellation of orbital data center satellites, though financial specifics were not disclosed.

title: Some European Launcher Challenge funding remains in limbo
summary: Nearly €140 million ($162 million) in European Launcher Challenge funding remains unallocated and risks being lost. The delay threatens to hinder European launch vehicle development programs.

title: Artemis 2 returns to the pad for April launch attempt
summary: Artemis 2, NASA's crewed lunar mission, has been rolled back to the launch pad in preparation for a potential April launch. The mission aims to send astronauts around the Moon as part of the Artemis program.

title: Officina Stellare wins $2 million contract for lasercom ground station in Spain
summary: Officina Stellare secured a $2 million contract to build a laser communication ground station in Spain for ICFO. The station will support future laser and quantum-encrypted space-to-Earth communications.

title: Rocket Lab launches eighth Synspective radar imaging satellite
summary: Rocket Lab launched the eighth radar imaging satellite for Japanese company Synspective on March 20. This mission marks another step in expanding Synspective's Earth observation capabilities.

title: Another GPS launch shifts from ULA to SpaceX as Vulcan investigation continues
summary: A GPS satellite launch has been reassigned from ULA's Vulcan Centaur to SpaceX due to ongoing investigations into the Vulcan rocket. This marks another shift in launch providers amid delays.

title: OHB Sweden wins €248 million contract to build EPS-Sterna constellation
summary: OHB Sweden secured a €248 million contract to build 20 satellites for Europe's EPS-Sterna constellation, enhancing weather forecasting and climate monitoring. This marks the largest contract ever for Sweden’s space sector.

title: Holistic space observation: the shift from SSA to SDA
summary: SpaceX's proposal to deploy up to one million satellites in low Earth orbit, combined with AI-enabled autonomous orbital infrastructure, signals a major shift toward large-scale, strategic space operations. This move reflects a broader industry trend toward holistic space observation, transitioning from Space Situational Awareness (SSA) to Space Domain Awareness (SDA).

title: ESA to fly dedicated Crew Dragon mission to ISS
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will charter a SpaceX Crew Dragon mission to the International Space Station (ISS) to provide additional flight opportunities for its astronauts.

title: 10 iconic William Shatner 'Star Trek' moments to celebrate his 95th birthday
summary: Celebrates William Shatner's 95th birthday with ten iconic moments from his 'Star Trek' career. The article highlights signature scenes from the franchise's history.

title: See the delicate crescent moon dance with the 'Seven Sisters' of the Pleiades tonight
summary: The Pleiades star cluster, also known as the 'Seven Sisters,' will be visible near the crescent moon tonight. This open star cluster in the constellation Taurus consists of around 1,000 stars.

title: What if the next great astronomer isn't human? How AI is revolutionizing our study of the cosmos
summary: AI is transforming astronomy by enabling new discoveries that may surpass human capabilities. The collaboration between human astronomers and AI is unlocking unprecedented insights into the cosmos.

title: 'This is really intolerable': Astronomers protest giant orbiting mirror project and SpaceX's million AI satellites
summary: Astronomers are protesting a proposed project involving giant orbiting mirrors and SpaceX's potential deployment of a million AI satellites, warning that these constellations would severely disrupt the night sky. Experts argue that such developments would irreparably alter humanity's view of the cosmos.

title: Russian cargo spacecraft suffers glitch after launching toward International Space Station
summary: A Russian Progress 94 cargo spacecraft experienced a glitch after launch, failing to deploy one of its docking antennas, which may delay its docking with the International Space Station.

title: We chat to legendary space shuttle commander Eileen Collins about her new documentary, 'Spacewoman' (interview)
summary: A new documentary titled 'Spacewoman' highlights the achievements of astronaut Eileen Collins, a legendary space shuttle commander. The film features an interview with Collins discussing her pioneering career.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 202 — Artemis Imminent
summary: Episode 202 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses the latest updates on NASA's Artemis program with guest Mike Wall, Space.com's spaceflight and tech editor.

title: Artemis Accords nations grapple with how to handle emergencies and 'harmful interference' on the moon
summary: Countries involved in the Artemis Accords are addressing challenges related to emergencies and potential harmful interference during lunar missions as moon exploration intensifies. The discussions aim to establish protocols for managing competing operations on the moon.

title: The loneliest places in the universe might actually be some of the best places for life
summary: Extreme exomoons with tidal heating and hydrogen-rich atmospheres could sustain billions of years of habitable conditions, challenging the idea that isolated places in the universe are lifeless.

title: Would you like to know more about 'Starship Troopers: Ultimate Bug War!'? Developer Auroch Digital has the answers (interview)
summary: Developer Auroch Digital discusses the latest Starship Troopers video game, highlighting its unique twists and strengths compared to previous installments. The interview provides insights into the game's development and features.

title: Transformational Tools and Technologies Resources
summary: NASA has released a series of studies, including Transformational Tools and Technologies Resources Vision Studies and Analysis of Alternatives (AoA) Studies, along with white papers to guide future space technology development and exploration strategies.

title: NASA’s X-59 Experimental Supersonic Aircraft Makes Second Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 experimental supersonic aircraft completed its second flight, marking the start of numerous test flights planned for 2026. Despite an early landing due to a technical issue, the team gathered valuable data and ensured a safe landing.

title: Hangar One Restoration Project
summary: Restoration of Hangar One, a historic aviation landmark in the San Francisco Bay Area, has been completed. The project was finalized by Planetary Ventures in December 2025.

title: NASA Selects University Finalists for Technology Concepts Competition
summary: NASA selected 14 university teams as finalists for the 2026 RASC-AL Competition, challenging students to design innovative concepts for human exploration on the Moon, Mars, and beyond. The competition aims to foster innovation by linking academia with the aerospace community.

title: How Open NASA Data on Comet 3I/ATLAS Will Power Tomorrow’s Discoveries
summary: NASA's public data archives will preserve observations of the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS, captured by over a dozen missions, as it exits our solar system. This data will fuel future scientific discoveries.

title: Smiles and Spacesuits
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams undergoes spacesuit fit verification aboard the International Space Station to ensure airtightness and mobility. The procedure also assesses comfort and mitigates safety risks.

title: NASA Exploration, Science Inspire “Project Hail Mary” Film
summary: NASA's upcoming Artemis II mission and the premiere of the film 'Project Hail Mary' highlight the intersection of real-life space exploration and science fiction. The film's release coincides with NASA's progress toward crewed missions to Mars.

title: NASA Simulations Improve Artemis II Launch Environment
summary: NASA used advanced simulations to optimize airflow around the Artemis II Space Launch System (SLS) rocket during its flight to the Moon. The simulations were conducted using a NASA-developed tool called the Launch, Ascent, and Vehicle Aerodynamics.

title: NASA Glenn Opens Applications for Free Summer Engineering Institute
summary: NASA Glenn Research Center is accepting applications for its free 2026 High School Engineering Institute, offering hands-on aerospace learning for rising juniors and seniors. Applications open March 20 and close May 1.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 successfully launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from the repaired Baikonur Cosmodrome pad. The mission follows repairs made after the previous Soyuz MS-28 launch from the same site.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center in space. This marks a significant expansion of the company's ambitions beyond launch services.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have been transported to the launch pad in preparation for the upcoming Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the historic lunar mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. The development brings the mission one step closer to launch.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of space launches is scheduled, featuring Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets. Nine launches are expected from various providers.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced a new mission concept called NEO Hunter, aimed at planetary defense against near-Earth objects. The hybrid concept combines various technologies to detect and mitigate potential asteroid threats.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans for Moon and Mars missions with the development of Starship at its Starfactory facility. The company is transforming steel production processes to support its ambitious space exploration goals.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: The International Space Station (ISS) welcomed new crew members and conducted science activities in February after Crew-11's early departure in mid-January due to a medical concern. The event highlights ongoing human spaceflight operations and research aboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to dominate another busy week of missions
summary: Eight launches are scheduled worldwide this week, with SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket dominating the launch schedule. The upcoming missions highlight the rocket's continued dominance in the space industry.

title: SpaceX’s Enters a new era with Booster 19 Testing on Pad 2
summary: SpaceX has begun testing Booster 19 on Pad 2, marking a new phase after Flight 11 in October 2025. This follows a 147-day gap since the last mission.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over the past 50 years. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and has 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting a series of events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25). These events aim to inform industry about key priorities and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. The visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will introduce ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its suppliers to use the software for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky and expensive solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Mission teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for testing robotic and human movement. This initiative aims to advance space technology and education.

title: SpaceX v. Amazon Heats Up at the FCC
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are engaged in a regulatory dispute at the FCC regarding their satellite constellations. The public dueling highlights the competitive tensions between the two companies in the satellite communications sector.

title: Kayhan Space Launches Satcat Terminal
summary: Kayhan Space has launched a new Satcat Terminal business, which may appeal to liberal arts majors. The initiative expands their offerings in the space industry.

title: Inside Austria’s Small But Mighty Space Ecosystem
summary: Austria's space industry is experiencing growth with increased funding and startup activity. The country is emerging as a notable player in the global space ecosystem.

title: Portal, Paladin Team On Debris Removal Service
summary: Portal and Paladin are collaborating on a debris removal service, aiming to transition debris management from experimental to operational. The initiative emphasizes the importance of satellite data for critical infrastructure like communications and national security.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its second suborbital flight of the SR75, scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026. The mission will take off from SaxaVord's launch site.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first satellite bus contract in Japan with NEC Corporation, which will purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission scheduled for next year.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: A modified version of the Vulcan rocket is expected to launch this summer after incorporating improvements to the nozzle and solid rocket booster. Changes were already planned to enhance margins before a recent anomaly.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round to reduce reliance on satellite infrastructure. The company aims to innovate in the space industry by offering alternative solutions.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics has secured a national security deal with South Korea, marking another step in its global expansion strategy. The company continues to find strong partnerships within South Korea's growing space industry.

title: FAA Ends Part 450 Transition Period
summary: The FAA has concluded the transition period for Part 450 licensing requirements, with all US launchers now operating under the updated five-year-old regulations. This marks the end of legacy regulations for the industry.

title: Spire Targets 50% Growth in 2026 After Adjustments in 2025
summary: Spire Global aims for 50% revenue growth in 2026 after divesting its maritime business and reducing debt in 2025, as announced by CEO Theresa Condor to investors.

title: Eutelsat CEO Fallacher Talks OneWeb Gen 2, Global Competition, and Sovereign Demand
summary: Eutelsat Group CEO Jean-François Fallacher discusses the company's strategic moves, including its involvement with OneWeb Gen 2, amidst global competition and sovereign demand. His tenure coincides with a transformative phase for Eutelsat.

title: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space Plan Repeatable Debris Removal Missions
summary: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space are collaborating to provide commercial debris removal missions under a recurring service model called Debris Removal as a Service (DRAAS). This partnership aims to address the growing issue of space debris through regular, commercialized cleanup operations.
company: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space

title: SLI Purchases Two Small GEO Satellites From ReOrbit
summary: SLI is set to purchase two small Geostationary Orbit (GEO) communication satellites from ReOrbit for 150 million euros ($172.8 million). The deal was announced in a joint statement by both companies.

title: Comtech Delivers First Set of Digital Modems to US Army
summary: Comtech delivered the first set of Enterprise Digital Intermediate Frequency Multi-Carrier (EDIM) modems to the U.S. Army under a $48.6 million contract signed in 2023. This delivery marks a significant step in modernizing the Army's communication infrastructure.

title: Latest White House Cybersecurity Strategy Talks Crypto, AI, Quantum
summary: The White House released President Trump's Cyber Strategy for America, emphasizing securing critical infrastructure and advancing emerging technologies like AI, quantum computing, and cryptocurrency. The strategy outlines six policy pillars to maintain U.S. superiority in cybersecurity.

title: Getting Better Outcomes In Space: How Will the UK Embrace the Challenge?
summary: A panel at Space-Comm in London discussed the security of UK space assets and strategic stability from government and industry perspectives.

title: SpiderOak Wins New UAS Systems Contract
summary: SpiderOak has secured a new contract from the U.S. Army Unmanned Aircraft Systems (PMO UAS) to conduct supply chain analysis and cybersecurity assessments for multiple vendors. The contract focuses on enhancing security and reliability in UAS systems.

title: Ukraine Ambassador to UK Talks Future Warfare
summary: The Ukrainian ambassador to the UK discussed the future of warfare during an event at Chatham House, marking four years since Russia's invasion. Valerii Zaluzhnyi highlighted key aspects of modern conflict and its evolution.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has filed a proposal with the FCC to host an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites, asserting that it will not cause interference. The company claims the SkyTerra Next proposal aligns with existing L-band coordination.
